name: azure-storage-blob-py
description: Azure Blob Storage SDK for Python. Use for uploading, downloading, listing blobs, managing containers, and blob lifecycle.
capabilities: Create and manage Azure Blob Storage containers
Upload blobs from files, bytes, or streams
Download blob content to files or memory
List and filter blobs with prefixes and hierarchies
Set blob metadata and content properties
Generate SAS tokens for temporary access
securitySummary: Prompt-only documentation skill providing reference material for Azure Blob Storage SDK for Python. No executable code scanned. Static analysis found zero issues. This skill contains only markdown documentation for helping users interact with Azure Blob Storage operations - no scripts, network calls, or file operations.
valueStatement: Need to manage cloud storage for your application? This skill provides the knowledge to upload, download, list, and manage Azure Blob Storage containers and blobs using the Python SDK.
userTitle: Work with Azure Blob Storage in Python
limitations: Does not provide Azure account or credentials - users must supply their own
Does not execute storage operations directly - provides guidance for AI
Does not include Azure Functions or Event Grid integration examples

useCases.targetUser: Data engineers building cloud data pipelines
useCases.title: Build data pipelines
useCases.description: Move data between local systems and cloud storage for ETL workflows

useCases.title: Serve static website assets
useCases.description: Host and serve static files like images, videos, and documents

useCases.title: Backup application data
useCases.description: Store backups of databases, logs, and application state

promptTemplates.title: Upload a file to blob storage
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me upload a file called 'data.json' to an Azure Blob Storage container named 'backups' using Python.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner user wanting to store a single file

promptTemplates.title: List all blobs in a container
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to list all blobs in a container called 'documents', including their sizes and last modified dates.
promptTemplates.scenario: User needs to inspect container contents

promptTemplates.title: Download large files efficiently
promptTemplates.prompt: Write Python code to download a 500MB file from blob storage with parallel downloading for better performance.

promptTemplates.title: Set up SAS token authentication
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a SAS token with read and write permissions that expires in 1 hour, and show how to use it to access a blob.
promptTemplates.scenario: User needs temporary access without full credentials

bestPractices: Use DefaultAzureCredential instead of connection strings for better security
Set appropriate content-type headers when uploading blobs for web serving
Use max_concurrency parameter for large file transfers to improve performance
antiPatterns: Storing account keys in code - always use Azure Identity or environment variables
Downloading entire large blobs into memory with readall() - use readinto() for streaming
Using hardcoded container or blob names - use configuration for flexibility

faq.question: Do I need an Azure account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need an Azure subscription and storage account. This skill provides the Python SDK knowledge but does not create or manage Azure resources.

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure Blob Storage?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential for managed identity or service principal authentication. Avoid connection strings when possible as they expose account keys.

faq.question: What's the difference between BlobServiceClient, ContainerClient, and BlobClient?
faq.answer: BlobServiceClient is for account-level operations. ContainerClient handles container operations like listing blobs. BlobClient is for single blob operations like upload and download.

faq.question: Can I upload files larger than 100MB?
faq.answer: Yes, Azure supports blobs up to 4.75TB. The SDK automatically uses block uploads for large files. Set max_single_put_size to control when block uploads begin.

faq.question: How do I generate a temporary access token?
faq.answer: Use generate_blob_sas() with your account key or user delegation key, specify permissions and expiry time. The returned token can be appended to the blob URL.

faq.question: Is there an async version of this SDK?
faq.answer: Yes, use azure.storage.blob.aio for async operations. Import BlobServiceClient from the aio module and use async/await patterns with context managers.
